message:
test(core/conformance): add empty violation goldens for filter_fidelity / metadata_redaction / privacy_redaction

Three new adapter-conformance violation goldens, all currently empty
(`[]`), covering v1 test cases that previously had no pinned
baseline:

* adapter_conformance_violations_filter_fidelity_v1.golden.json
* adapter_conformance_violations_metadata_redaction_v1.golden.json
* adapter_conformance_violations_privacy_redaction_v1.golden.json

Why empty now: the adapter conformance test harness generates a
violations report per scenario. "No violations" = "[]". Pinning the
empty baseline NOW means any future drift — a real detected violation
OR a bug that starts emitting spurious violations — shows up as a
golden diff on the next run rather than silently passing through a
"test exists but never asserted" hole.

These join the existing family of `adapter_conformance_violations_*_v1`
goldens (embedding_invalid, index_invalid, lifecycle_invalid,
resource_invalid, search_empty_query, search_result_count_exceeds_sources)
with the same v1 schema contract.

No code change, no adapter change — this purely expands the golden
surface area so the next adapter regression is caught by the v1
suite.
